decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader width to enhance lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Widening the heat spreader increases the cross-sectional area for lateral heat flow, reducing thermal resistance between the chip and the side boundaries held at 25°C.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader
risk_notes: The spreader width is approaching the design domain limit (1.2m); future geometry changes may be limited by boundary proximity.
Diminishing returns may occur as the spreader approaches the full width available between boundaries.
